weather.tip: It's already drizzling with a 96% rain chance holding through the evening — carry an umbrella for the commute home, add extra buffer time for waterlogged stretches, and avoid underpasses if the rain intensifies.

around.headline: 18-year-old girl stabbed by rejected suitor in Whitefield
around.summary: A man stabbed his ex-girlfriend after she rejected his marriage proposal and then tried to kill himself; she was hospitalised and he's in custody. If you're being persistently pursued after a breakup, document the messages/calls and report to police early rather than waiting for it to escalate.
around.source: NDTV / The Hindu / The New Indian Express

around.headline: Bengaluru police raids seize 165 vehicles, check rowdy-sheeters, book NDPS cases
around.summary: A citywide special police drive today checked known rowdy-sheeters, seized 165 vehicles and booked cases under the NDPS Act. It's a routine but visible enforcement push — expect more vehicle checks on arterial roads this week.

around.headline: PIB warns of fake traffic-challan links spreading on WhatsApp and SMS
around.summary: The Press Information Bureau flagged malicious WhatsApp/SMS messages posing as traffic e-challans that push victims to click a payment link. Never pay through a link in the message — verify any challan directly at echallan.parivahan.gov.in first.

around.headline: Karnataka outlines metro extensions and tunnel roads to tackle Bengaluru traffic
around.summary: State plans discussed today include further metro expansion alongside tunnel-road proposals to ease congestion, though these are multi-year infrastructure plans, not immediate fixes. Don't expect any near-term change to your commute from this.

around.headline: GBA seeks Supreme Court's nod to push Bengaluru civic body polls to December
around.summary: The Greater Bengaluru Authority has asked the Supreme Court to postpone local body elections to December, citing the ongoing Karnataka SIR (electoral roll revision). If you were expecting ward-level polls soon, they're likely delayed further.
around.source: The Hindu / The New Indian Express

around.headline: Iran-US tensions stay high; IEA flags risk to 2027 oil market surplus
around.summary: Iran says it struck US military targets in the Gulf and at a command centre in Jordan, and the IEA now warns the standoff could threaten a projected 2027 oil surplus. Worth watching pump prices if the conflict widens further.

events.name: Happy Streets, Whitefield
events.when: Sunday morning, check local listings for exact date
events.note: Car-free open-streets morning returns to Whitefield — good for walking, cycling, and street performances without traffic.

events.name: Bengaluru International Short Film Festival (BISFF)
events.note: Citywide short-film festival's main dates are now confirmed; a pre-show gala already took place ahead of it.

events.name: Three-day classical dance celebration
events.note: A three-day classical dance festival is on in the city — worth catching if you're into Bharatanatyam/Kuchipudi-style performances.

events.name: Ranga Shankara children's theatre festival
events.when: Returns this season, check venue listings
events.note: Ranga Shankara's annual theatre festival for children is back — a solid weekend option for families.

events.name: "Grief and loss through botanical art" exhibition
events.note: A botanical art exhibition exploring grief and loss is showing in the city — a quieter, reflective outing.

street_smart.title: A WhatsApp message says you have an unpaid traffic challan — pay now or your license gets suspended. Do you click?
street_smart.based_on: Real warning issued today: PIB flagged a fake traffic-challan scam circulating via malicious WhatsApp and SMS links.
street_smart.situation: You get a WhatsApp message that looks official — your vehicle number, a challan amount, and a link to 'pay now' before a deadline or risk your license being suspended. It looks urgent and specific enough to be real. What do you do?

choices.text: Click the link straight away and enter your card details to pay before the deadline mentioned in the message.
choices.feedback: This is the actual trap: the link leads to a fake payment page built to harvest your card number, expiry, CVV and OTP. Real challans never carry a personal countdown-deadline threat delivered over WhatsApp — that urgency is the manipulation, not a real rule.

choices.text: Call the number the message came from to 'confirm' whether the challan is genuine.
choices.feedback: You'd likely just be talking to the scammer or an accomplice, who will happily 'confirm' it's real. A callback to an unverified number the message itself gave you isn't independent verification.

choices.text: Ignore and delete the message immediately, assuming every such text is fake.
choices.feedback: Most are scams, but genuine unpaid e-challans do exist and can pile up fines or affect renewals if ignored. Blind dismissal isn't verification either — you still don't actually know your challan status this way.

choices.text: Don't click the link. Go directly to echallan.parivahan.gov.in (or the Karnataka traffic police app) and look up your vehicle number to check and pay if it's genuine.
choices.feedback: Correct. Government challans are always checkable independently on the official Parivahan portal, so you never need to trust an inbound link. Typing the address yourself removes the scammer from the transaction entirely.

takeaways: Never pay through a link sent to you in SMS/WhatsApp — go to echallan.parivahan.gov.in directly and search by your vehicle number instead.
Urgency and countdown deadlines ('pay in 24 hours or license suspended') are themselves a phishing signal — genuine regulatory processes don't threaten you over chat apps.
If you've already clicked and entered card details, block the card immediately via your bank's app/helpline and file a complaint at cybercrime.gov.in or call 1930.

quiz.q: How many vehicles were seized in today's special Bengaluru police drive targeting rowdy-sheeters?
quiz.why: The raid also booked NDPS (narcotics) cases — separate from the unrelated 1,581-vehicle abandoned-vehicle towing drive making news this week.

quiz.q: PIB warned today that a fake Bengaluru traffic-challan scam is spreading through which channels?
quiz.why: The safest move is to skip the link entirely and check any challan directly at echallan.parivahan.gov.in.

quiz.q: Reuters reported today that Andy Burnham is poised to become the next prime minister of which country?
quiz.why: Most Labour lawmakers have thrown their support behind him, per today's coverage of the UK Labour Party.

quiz.q: The Parivahan e-challan portal used to check and pay Indian traffic fines online is run by which central ministry?
options: Ministry of Road Transport and Highways
quiz.why: State and city police forces issue the challans, but MoRTH operates the shared digital portal used across all states.

quiz.q: In Indian business reporting, what does "GCC" stand for — as in today's coverage of Bengaluru's GCC hub?
quiz.why: GCCs are in-house offshore units multinationals set up in India for engineering, R&D and operations — distinct from third-party BPO/IT-services firms.
